verdict.answer_first: A: sentence 1 directly defines it: "structuring tables so that each fact lives in exactly one place"; B also answers early but starts with a heading.
verdict.decisiveness: A gives a cleaner rule: "normalize to 3NF first, then denormalize only where a real, measured performance problem justifies it"; B surveys more cases and adds more jargon.
verdict.fabrication: B overreaches with unsupported absolutes/claims like "almost always worth it" and "cut latency dramatically"; A's examples are illustrative and more restrained.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it ties each denormalization case to the tradeoff and closes with "measure before you trade away integrity."
